overview: The deck aims to generate massive amounts of mana, cast a high-impact Dragon, and then chain together multiple Dragon spells to trigger The Ur-Dragon’s ability, creating an exponentially growing army of flying, haste creatures to overwhelm opponents through combat or finish with a game-ending X-spell.
win_conditions: The Ur-Dragon itself (once you have 5+ mana, cast it and follow up with 2+ Dragons for a lethal army), massive X-spells, or token doublers to swing combat.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one mana acceleration piece (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, or ramp spell), and an early-game Dragon or removal.
Keep any hand with The Ur-Dragon if you have 2+ mana sources.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have 4+ lands with no acceleration, or 3+ acceleration with no lands.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Sol Ring/Arcane Signet, or cast a cheap ramp spell.
Turn 2: Cast a 2-3 mana Dragon or another ramp piece.
If you have 4 mana, cast a cheap Dragon to start triggering The Ur-Dragon.
Prioritize fixing your mana base early to ensure you can cast 5-color spells by turn 4-5.
mid_game: Hoard mana until you can cast a high-impact Dragon or a massive X-spell.
Use card draw to maintain hand size, as casting multiple Dragons per turn is key.
if you use exile effects, ensure you have recursion for key Dragons.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without protection or a clear win condition.
Commit threats when you have mana advantage or when the board is clear.
Hold back if opponents have sweepers or targeted removal.
Use flying and haste to bypass ground blockers.
late_game: The Ur-Dragon itself (once you have 5+ mana, cast it and follow up with 2+ Dragons for a lethal army), massive X-spells, or token doublers to swing combat.
Go all-in when you have 10+ mana, a clear path to combat, and no immediate sweepers in play.
Commit when you can cast The Ur-Dragon and 2+ Dragons in a single turn, or when you have a guaranteed lethal with your current board state.

sequencing_tips: The Ur-Dragon's ability triggers on casting, not entering, so you can cast Dragons from exile or the graveyard if you have recursion.
Avoid casting Dragons with X in your mana pool unless you're ready to commit
you can't change X after casting.
Flying and haste make your tokens highly evasive, but be wary of board wipes.
Always track opponents' mana to anticipate sweepers or counterspells.
Common mistake: overcommitting to the board without protection or a clear win condition, leading to a wiped board and no comeback.

matchups.advice: Use cantrips and cheap Dragons to trade up. Cast Dragons with haste or flying to bypass counters if possible. Use mana rocks to cast multiple spells in a turn, forcing counterspells to be spent on non-essential pieces. Consider bringing in protection or alternative win conditions.

Apply early pressure with flying Dragons and removal. Don't let them get to their massive finishers. Use board wipes to reset their board. If they out-ramp you, focus on card advantage and recursion to stay in the game.
Stabilize with early removal and board wipes. Use flying Dragons to trade efficiently. Don't overextend; hold back mana for interaction. Once stabilized, pivot to your combo/finisher.
Pressure with cheap Dragons and cantrips to out-value their removal. Use card draw to out-resource them. If they lock you out, use alternative win conditions or bring in additional finishers.

Win on card advantage and board presence. Prioritize drawing extra cards and protecting The Ur-Dragon. Use targeted removal to eliminate their key Dragons. If they go wide, use sweepers or targeted removal to clear the board before striking.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes, targeted removal on The Ur-Dragon, and combo win conditions.
Pay attention to opponents' mana bases
if they're holding up blue, assume counterspells.
Track graveyard hate if you rely on recursion.
